wang-v-moutidis-2025-08-18.lesson: When opposing a pro-se litigant's AI-fabricated quotes, demand the primary exhibit to expose the fabrication fast.

ploni-v-ashdod-port-company-ltd-2025-08-.lesson: Israeli courts will impose costs on pro-se AI misuse even without fabricated-citation detail on record.

jne24-v-minister-for-immigration-and-cit.lesson: Consumer tools like Claude or Copilot in immigration filings trigger personal costs and Bar referral — mandate approved-tools list.

kuzniar-v-general-dental-council-2025-08.lesson: Even when costs are declined, a pro-se ChatGPT bundle with 28 bad authorities shows why opposing counsel must Shepardize everything.

maxwell-v-westjet-airlines-ltd-2025-08-1.lesson: ChatGPT will cite repealed statutes as live law; verify every statutory citation against the current code, not AI output.

yashcheshen-v-saskatchewan-government-in.lesson: Denying AI use when confronted does not erase a fabricated SCC quote — denial only deepens the court's adverse inference.

director-of-public-prosecutions-v-gr-202.lesson: Joint submissions require independent verification by each firm — do not assume opposing counsel vetted the AI-drafted citations.

holloway-v-beckles-2025-08-12.lesson: UK tribunals now treat fabricated citations as unreasonable conduct sufficient for costs — flag AI risks in every pro-se matter.

ms-bangladesh-2025-08-12.lesson: Handing the Panel ChatGPT-fabricated 'Y (China)' printouts triggered BSB referral — ban consumer AI from appellate immigration bundles.

meital-kasantini-v-hagivaa-proyectim-han.lesson: Admitting AI fabrication post-filing still costs you money and your evidence — verify before the statement of claim goes out.

blinds-to-go-inc-c-blachley-2025-08-11.lesson: Courts now spot hallucinations by the tell of 'cited but not filed' — require the actual judgment copy for every authority.

licksun-company-limited-v-occupiers-of-l.lesson: Delegating authority lists to non-legal staff is no defence when six of six cases are fictitious — lawyers sign the list, lawyers verify it.

musselman-v-vanderstelt-2025-08-08.lesson: BC appellate courts now cite hallucinations as a factor granting security for costs — raise it early in your response materials.

deysel-v-electra-lift-co-2025-08-08.lesson: ChatGPT's advice to commence unfounded applications past limitation kills cases — warn clients that AI is not legal counsel.

may-v-costaras-2025-08-08.lesson: NSW Court of Appeal flagged the need for judicial vigilance — assume any pro-se authority list contains at least one hallucination.

gadban-v-israel-land-authority-2025-08-0.lesson: Thirty fabricated judgments in one brief is career-risk territory — enforce 100% citation sampling on any AI-drafted pleading.

pinchas-v-supervisor-of-insolvency-2025-.lesson: Withdrawing the motion does not purge the sanction — Israeli courts punish the filing, not just reliance at hearing.

marcus-groesser-and-ira-hess-v-robert-ph.lesson: Speaking notes are submissions — courts reject the 'not on the official record' defence and will refer to the Bar.

halton-regional-municipality-v-rewa-et-a.lesson: AI will invent whole legal doctrines ('train of inquiry' disclosure) — verify the proposition exists, not just the citation.

the-father-v-the-mother-2025-07-30.lesson: Family courts now treat AI-faked authorities as litigation misconduct warranting costs — flag to every self-represented opposing party.
